notes: Full ADR history lives in docs/DECISIONS.md (ADR-0001 through ADR-0028) — this field now tracks only current, load-bearing facts for picking up work. cmd/devlab/main.go (no flags) bootstraps config -> storage -> workspace.Manager -> template.Registry -> runtimes -> service/factory -> engine.Engine -> api server and listens on config.ListenAddr (devlab IS the REST API server process, ADR-0026); --version prints the version and exits. api/ (Fiber v2) is a thin, business-logic-free HTTP layer over Engine (ADR-0027): GET /healthz; GET /api/templates[/:name]; POST /api/workspaces; GET /api/workspaces[/:id]; DELETE /api/workspaces/:id; POST /api/workspaces/:id/{start,stop,reset}; GET /api/workspaces/:id/{status,logs}. No authentication yet (Sprint 15). engine.New takes (*workspace.Manager, *template.Registry, *factory.Factory). CreateWorkspace only creates the Workspace record; StartWorkspace lazily provisions+starts each attached Service, idempotently (ADR-0025). workspace.Manager.SetStatus/DataDir support this. internal/service.Service is the shared Service contract; internal/service also owns the service-type catalog (KnownTypes/IsKnownType) used by both internal/template (validation) and internal/service/factory (construction). internal/service/factory.Build maps a service type + workspace ID + data dir to a concrete Service: kubernetes -> k3d-backed; docker -> Docker-in-Docker (privileged, named-volume storage); jenkins -> jenkins.New with a dynamically allocated free port; linux/terraform/ansible -> a clear not-implemented-yet error. utils.NewID is 12 hex chars (not 32) to fit k3d's 32-character cluster name limit. Next candidate work: web/ Next.js Dashboard (Sprint 12).
